skill.name: redesign-existing-projects
skill.description: Audits existing websites and apps, then applies focused visual upgrades using the current framework and styling system.
skill.summary: Improve an existing web project with focused typography, layout, color, state, and content upgrades.
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many shell and cryptography indicators, but the shell matches are markdown inline examples and the cryptography matches are content false positives. The real concerns are normal for this skill type: it directs broad repository edits and recommends an external placeholder image service.

medium_findings.title: Broad Repository Modification Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill tells the agent to read the codebase and apply design upgrades in the existing stack. This is legitimate for a redesign skill, but it can alter application behavior if changes are not reviewed and tested.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file directly instructs scanning and modifying projects. The same section also tells users to preserve the current stack and test changes, which reduces but does not remove risk.

medium_findings.title: External Placeholder Image Source
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends a third-party placeholder image URL when real assets are unavailable. Use of that URL can introduce external requests and third-party content into generated projects.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is explicitly present and may be copied into project output. It appears to be a benign placeholder source, not a data exfiltration endpoint.

low_findings.title: Shell Execution Detections Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the cited lines use markdown inline code for CSS values, HTML tags, and design examples. No executable script or command invocation is present in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited syntax is inside a markdown document and is used for formatting examples. I found no Ruby file, shell script, or instruction to execute the inline snippets as commands.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are Content False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm indicators, but the cited lines contain frontmatter, headings, and design guidance. No cryptographic API, hash function, cipher, or key handling appears in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is visible design prose, not code. No evidence found for weak cryptographic algorithms or cryptographic operations.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Detections Are Benign Design Terms
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported reconnaissance patterns, but the cited lines discuss CSS layout, stock imagery, and broken grid design. No host, network, process, or environment discovery behavior is described.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are ordinary visual design recommendations. I found no evidence of commands or instructions for system discovery.

content.user_title: Redesign Existing Projects With Premium UI
content.value_statement: Existing web apps often look generic after fast AI or template-driven builds. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured redesign checklist for focused visual upgrades.
actual_capabilities: Audits typography, color, layout, spacing, component patterns, and interaction states.
Identifies common AI-generated design patterns that make interfaces look generic.
Guides focused improvements within the existing framework or CSS approach.
Prioritizes upgrades by impact, including fonts, palettes, states, and layout cleanup.
Encourages accessibility basics such as focus rings, alt text, skip links, and semantic HTML.
Supports Tailwind, vanilla CSS, styled components, and other existing styling systems.
limitations: It does not provide a complete brand strategy or visual identity system.
It may change project files, so review diffs and test the app after use.
It recommends third-party placeholder imagery when real assets are unavailable.
It depends on the agent's ability to inspect and understand the current project.

use_cases.title: Refresh a prototype before a demo
use_cases.description: Turn a functional prototype into a more polished interface without rebuilding the app.

use_cases.title: Improve a generated frontend
use_cases.description: Remove generic AI design patterns and add more deliberate typography, layout, and states.

use_cases.title: Audit product UI consistency
use_cases.description: Find weak visual hierarchy, missing states, spacing issues, and accessibility gaps across an existing app.

prompt_templates.title: Basic visual audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of an existing app
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this project using the redesign-existing-projects skill. List the most visible typography, spacing, color, layout, and state problems before editing anything.

prompt_templates.title: Focused landing page polish
prompt_templates.prompt: Redesign the main landing page with this skill. Keep the current framework and content structure, but improve hierarchy, visual depth, spacing, and responsive behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Application interface upgrade
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply this skill to the app interface. Prioritize dense, readable layouts, clear navigation states, accessible forms, loading states, empty states, and consistent component spacing.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced design system cleanup
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to audit the design system and implementation patterns. Replace inconsistent colors, type scales, shadows, radii, icon use, and component states with focused improvements.

output_examples.input: Audit my dashboard UI and improve the first screen.
output: A concise design audit covering typography, spacing, layout, states, and component issues.
Targeted project changes that improve hierarchy, readability, and interaction feedback.
A short verification note describing what changed and what still needs review.

output_examples.input: Make this marketing page feel less generic.
output: A redesigned page with stronger type scale, improved imagery, adjusted color treatment, and less repetitive card structure.
Updated copy where placeholder names, generic claims, and weak calls to action were visible.
Responsive checks for layout stability and text fit.

output_examples.input: Polish this Tailwind project without changing the stack.
output: Focused edits using the existing Tailwind setup and project conventions.
Improved hover, focus, active, loading, empty, and error states where relevant.
A brief summary of changed components and any recommended follow-up testing.

best_practices: Start with an audit before asking the agent to edit files.
Review visual diffs and run the existing test or build command after changes.
Provide real brand assets, copy, and images when available.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a full rewrite when the goal is visual polish.
Do not accept external placeholder images in production without review.
Do not skip accessibility states such as focus, error, loading, and empty states.

faq.question: What does this skill improve?
faq.answer: It improves visual design quality in existing websites and apps, including typography, layout, color, states, and content details.

faq.question: Does it work with Tailwind?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill tells the agent to inspect the project and work with the existing styling system, including Tailwind.

faq.question: Will it rewrite my project?
faq.answer: No. The instructions emphasize targeted upgrades and keeping the current framework and styling approach.

faq.question: Is it safe for production code?
faq.answer: Use it with normal code review. It can edit project files and may suggest external placeholder imagery.

faq.question: Can it improve accessibility?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for focus rings, semantic HTML, alt text, skip links, and form validation states.

faq.question: Does it create original brand assets?
faq.answer: No. It can guide visual polish, but it does not replace a complete brand identity or asset creation process.
